name: Homemade Tomato Soup I
recipeInstructions:
1. Core the tomatoes and chop them coarsely.
2. Chop the onions, peppers, celery, and parsley coarsely, and divide equally along with the tomatoes between 2 very large, deep kettles. Divide the salt between the 2 kettles as well.
3. Cover and bring to a boil, then simmer over medium-low heat for 1 hour, or until all vegetables are very tender. Stir frequently.
4. Put the mixture through a sieve or food mill. Transfer the puree to a large kettle.
5. In a medium bowl, combine all remaining ingredients except butter.
6. Add the seasonings gradually to the hot puree, beating in with a whisk.
7. Add the butter and whisk smooth. Bring to a boil, then simmer over medium-low heat for 3 minutes.
8. Pour hot into 12 hot pint jars, leaving 1/2 inch head space. Seal and process in hot water bath for 15 minutes.
9. To serve, add one 11-ounce can cream of mushroom soup and 1 soup can of water to every pint of tomato soup base. Heat and serve.
Yield: 6 to 8 servings.
